description: Create the i18n infrastructure: LanguageContext, locale JSON files (ko.json, en.json), useTranslation hook, SEO head manager component, language-prefixed routing (/ko/, /en/), and KOR/ENG toggle in desktop menu and mobile hamburger menu. This feature establishes all foundational pieces so subsequent features can simply use the translation hook.
steps: 1. Create grape/src/locales/ko.json with all Korean text organized by section keys (nav, footer, home, solutions, rnd, products, cases, about, login, common, seo). Extract ALL text from: Menu items (defaultMenuItems in Menu/index.jsx), Footer text (Footer/index.jsx sitemapData + bottom info), Home page (Home.jsx cards data, hero section), breadcrumbs, page titles, subtitles, button labels, form labels, error messages. The JSON should be deeply nested by page/component.

2. Create grape/src/locales/en.json with professional business English translations for the AI safety management domain. Menu: Company, Business Areas, R&D, Products, Client Cases, Government Programs, Shopping Mall, Admin. Use domain-appropriate terms: 'Smart Safety Platform', 'On-Device AI', 'Multimodal Situational Awareness', 'Integrated Control System', 'RAG-based LLM', 'Early Risk Detection', 'Cognitive Disability Assistance', 'AI Air Quality Management', etc.

3. Create grape/src/contexts/LanguageContext.jsx - a React context that: (a) reads initial language from URL path prefix (/ko/ or /en/), falling back to localStorage 'lang' key, defaulting to 'ko'; (b) provides currentLang, setLanguage(lang), t(key) function for translations; (c) persists selection to localStorage; (d) the t() function should support nested dot-notation keys (e.g., t('nav.company')) and return the key itself as fallback if not found.
4. Create grape/src/hooks/useTranslation.js - a convenience hook that wraps useContext(LanguageContext) and returns { t, currentLang, setLanguage }.

5. Create grape/src/components/SEOHead/index.jsx - a component using document.title and meta tag manipulation (or react-helmet-like approach with useEffect) to set: document title, meta description, og:title, og:description, og:locale (ko_KR or en_US), og:url, hreflang alternate links, canonical URL based on current language and path.

6. Modify grape/src/main.jsx - wrap BrowserRouter with a basename approach OR modify App.jsx routing to support /ko/* and /en/* prefixes. The recommended approach: keep BrowserRouter as-is, add a LanguageProvider that wraps AuthProvider in App.jsx, and handle language from URL path prefix inside LanguageContext using useLocation.

7. Modify grape/src/App.jsx - (a) wrap everything in LanguageProvider; (b) add route structure that supports both /ko/... and /en/... prefixes plus bare paths (which redirect to /ko/...); (c) include SEOHead component at the top level; (d) create a LanguageRouter wrapper component that extracts lang from URL prefix path and sets it in context.

8. Modify grape/src/components/Menu/index.jsx - (a) import useTranslation; (b) replace hardcoded Korean menu labels with t('nav.xxx') calls; (c) add a KOR/ENG language toggle button/switch in the menu bar (positioned after the logout button area or before menu items); (d) clicking the toggle should call setLanguage and navigate to the equivalent path with the new language prefix.
9. Modify grape/src/components/HamburgerMenu/index.jsx - add KOR/ENG toggle in the mobile menu (at the top near the logo, or as a prominent toggle). Style it for mobile touch targets.
10. Modify grape/src/components/Footer/index.jsx - replace all hardcoded Korean text with t() calls for sitemap labels, address, copyright text.

11. Ensure the language prefix approach works: visiting /en/about/company shows English, /ko/about/company shows Korean, bare /about/company redirects to /ko/about/company. The language toggle on any page navigates to the same path but with the other language prefix.
12. Run vite build to confirm no compilation errors.
acceptance_criteria: grape/src/locales/ko.json exists with comprehensive Korean text for all menu items, footer, home page, page titles, breadcrumbs, and common UI text
grape/src/locales/en.json exists with professional English translations
LanguageContext provides t(), currentLang, setLanguage and persists to localStorage
Desktop menu shows KOR/ENG toggle and all labels come from translation files
Mobile hamburger menu shows KOR/ENG toggle
Footer text comes from translation files
SEOHead component updates document title, meta description, og tags, and hreflang based on language
findings: Build succeeds without errors. The i18n routing uses /ko/* and /en/* Route paths in App.jsx with a LanguageRedirect fallback for bare paths. LanguageContext is only rendered inside language-prefixed routes, so it always sees the correct lang from URL. Menu, HamburgerMenu, and Footer all use t() for labels. Internal Link components in page content still use absolute paths without language prefix - they will trigger LanguageRedirect (works but not optimal), and should be updated in FEAT-002 when page content is translated. The main.jsx file was unchanged (BrowserRouter stays as-is). The desktop menu CSS uses 'menu-lang-toggle' class and hamburger menu uses 'hamburger-lang-toggle' class for the KOR/ENG buttons.
